QID305: Journalists are using AI in their articles mainly to save time and improve efficiency, especially when handling large amounts of data or breaking news. AI can help with tasks like summarizing information, generating drafts, and fact-checking, allowing reporters to focus more on analysis and storytelling. It also enables newsrooms to produce content faster and keep up with the high demand for constant updates. However, human oversight is still important to ensure accuracy, credibility, and ethical reporting.

QID305: I believe journalists are increasingly turning to AI because it acts as a high-speed research assistant, capable of scanning massive datasets or long reports to find "the needle in the haystack" in seconds. QID305: Most journalists are not using AI, and are extremely against any use of generative AI in journalism. However, the few who use it are doing so because editors are placing an inordinate amount of work on them that they could never be able to do without using a cheat code of some sort. The work is compromised, and sources are often lost in the process, but we are living in an era where high-volume productivity is prioritized over all. It's an extremely dangerous game to play because it undermines credibility with sources, erodes trust with readers, and creates newsroom divisions. At the end of the day, it's not an editor's name on new stories, it's the name of the writer. The editor and the company may be forcing AI against the will of the writer, but when a mistake is made because of AI, aren't the ones who garner the blame or the consequences.

QID305: I believe journalists are generally caught in between opinions on AI. In one sense, most see the obvious benefits it can bring with fact-checking, suggestions of improvements etc. But on the other, there's a fear that AI will replace the need for journalists in time and that by using it to assist with work, journalists are in some way slitting their own throats. I also think there's an overriding feeling that AI is not currently reliable enough for things like fact-checking and data to fully 'rely' upon it. Journalists are taught ethical values at length during training so I believe most journalists would be keen to disclose any AI use in articles.
